info.title: Pay with me API
info.summary: Read and write a user's shared-expense groups, transactions and balances.
info.description:
A versioned REST/JSON API over Pay with me's shared-expense engine, meant for
scripts and AI agents acting on a user's behalf.

- **Auth**: `Authorization: Bearer <api-key>` on every request. Keys are minted
  in the app under Settings → API keys. A key sees exactly the groups its owner
  sees; there is no separate API identity.
- **Scopes**: `read` keys may call every `GET`; write operations additionally
  require a `write` key (otherwise `403 forbidden_scope`).
- **JSON only**: every request and response body is `application/json`.
- **Money**: every monetary value is a self-describing
  `{ "amount": <integer minor units>, "currency": "<ISO code>" }` object. Never a
  float, never a formatted string. Discover each currency's minor-unit exponent
  and symbol once via `GET /currencies`.
- **Errors**: every error is `{ "error": { "code", "message", "details"? } }`.
- **CORS is closed** — this API is server-to-server; it is not callable from a
  browser page.

Group, member and invite management are **not** exposed in v1: use the web app.

servers.description: Version 1 (relative to whatever origin serves the app).

tags.name: Groups
tags.description: The groups the calling key's owner belongs to.

tags.name: Transactions
tags.description: Read and write a group's transactions.

get.description:
The supported-currency reference table. This is where a client discovers each
currency's `exponent` (how many minor units make one major unit — 2 for `USD`,
0 for `JPY`) and `symbol`, so that the money objects on the wire can stay small.
Any valid key may call this.

Every group the key's owner is an active member of. Unpaginated — only the
transactions list paginates. A group the caller cannot see simply never appears.

A group the caller can see. A group that does not exist and a group the caller
has no access to are deliberately indistinguishable: both are `404 not_found`.

The full roster, including deactivated members (a member with a non-null
`deactivatedAt` keeps their history but can no longer be added to a new
transaction). Member ids are what every payer / beneficiary / balance refers to.
Unpaginated.

One row per member: Σ paid − Σ owed, in the group's **settlement currency**.
Positive = the group owes them (creditor); negative = they owe the group
(debtor); zero = square. Unpaginated.

The group's transactions, newest first, ordered by `(createdAt DESC, occurredAt
DESC, id)`. This is the only paginated collection: pass the `nextCursor` from a
response back as `cursor` to fetch the next page; a `null` `nextCursor` means
you have reached the end. The cursor is **opaque** — do not construct or parse
it (an undecodable one is a `400 bad_request`).

Soft-deleted transactions are excluded from this list; they remain fetchable by
id (with `deletedAt` set) and can be restored.

post.description:
Records a spending or a transfer. Requires a `write` key.

The body is the complete transaction input (the same payload the web app
submits). Note in particular:

- `amountTotal` is in **`currency`'s** minor units; `amountTotalSettlement` is
  the same money expressed in the **group's settlement currency**, and you must
  supply it. It is re-checked server-side and must equal exactly
  `round_half_up(amountTotal × exchangeRate × 10^(exp_settlement − exp_currency))`,
  where `exp_x` is the currency's `exponent` from `GET /currencies`. A mismatch
  is a `422 validation_error` naming `amountTotalSettlement`.
- Same-currency is the trivial case: `exchangeRate: "1"` and
  `amountTotalSettlement == amountTotal`.
- `Σ payers[].amountPaid` must equal `amountTotal`.

Send an `Idempotency-Key` header (strongly recommended): a retry with the same
key and the same body replays the original response instead of creating a second
transaction.

equalSplit.summary: An equal-split spending in the settlement currency

foreignCurrency.summary: A foreign-currency spending (JPY entry, THB settlement @ 0.244)

get.description:
The full detail: payers, resolved per-member shares (in the settlement currency),
itemized lines and charges. A soft-deleted transaction is still returned, with
`deletedAt` set.

put.description:
A **full replacement** (hence `PUT`, not `PATCH`): send the complete transaction
input, exactly as for a create. Any field you omit is not "left alone" — it is
replaced by the schema default or rejected. Requires a `write` key.

Concurrency is **last-write-wins**: there is no version / `If-Match` in v1, so a
`PUT` built from a stale read silently reverts anything changed in between. Read
immediately before writing.

A soft-deleted transaction cannot be edited — restore it first (`422`).

delete.description:
Soft-deletes the transaction: it stops counting towards balances and drops out of
the list, but stays fetchable by id and can be restored. Requires a `write` key.

Idempotent: deleting an already-deleted transaction succeeds with `200` and
writes no new audit entry.

post.description:
Un-deletes the transaction: it counts towards balances again. Requires a `write`
key. Idempotent: restoring a live transaction succeeds with `200` and writes no
new audit entry. Takes no request body.

Sugar over `POST …/transactions`: records `from` paying `to` as a single-payer /
single-beneficiary transfer in the group's **settlement currency** (rate 1),
under the "Debt settlement" category. Requires a `write` key.

`amount` is a plain integer in the group's settlement-currency minor units — the
currency is group context, so it is neither sent nor trusted from the payload.
The response is the created transfer, in the same shape as any other transaction.

Send an `Idempotency-Key` header so a retry cannot record the payment twice.

bearerAuth.description:
An API key minted in the app (Settings → API keys), sent as
`Authorization: Bearer <key>`. The full key is shown exactly once, at creation.
Every missing / invalid / expired / revoked key collapses to the same
`401 unauthorized`.

Limit.name: limit
Limit.description: Page size. Out-of-range values are rejected (`422`), not clamped.

Cursor.name: cursor
Cursor.description: An opaque `nextCursor` from a previous page. Do not construct or parse it.

FromFilter.name: from
FromFilter.description: Inclusive lower bound on the transaction's real-world date (`createdAt`).

ToFilter.name: to
ToFilter.description: Inclusive upper bound on the transaction's real-world date (`createdAt`).

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description:
Optional but **strongly recommended** on creates. A client-chosen unique string,
remembered for 24h and scoped to the calling key:

- same key + same body → the stored response is replayed (no second transaction,
  no second audit entry);
- same key + a different body → `409 conflict` (`details.reason: "key_reused"`);
- a concurrent retry that loses the race → `409 conflict`
  (`details.reason: "in_progress"`).

Without the header a create is at-least-once: a retry after a network timeout may
record the transaction twice.

Money.description:
A monetary value. `amount` is an integer in `currency`'s **minor units** — never a
float, never a formatted string. `฿450.00` is `{ "amount": 45000, "currency": "THB" }`;
`¥3600` (a 0-exponent currency) is `{ "amount": 3600, "currency": "JPY" }`. Get each
currency's `exponent` from `GET /currencies`.

amount.description: The value in `currency`'s minor units. May be negative (e.g. a balance).

Currency.description: One supported currency and how to format it.

exponent.description: Minor units per major unit, as a power of ten. `2` for `THB`, `0` for `JPY`.

settlementCurrency.description: The currency every balance and settlement amount in this group is expressed in.

createdBy.description: The user id of the group's creator.

id.description: The id every payer / beneficiary / balance refers to.

userId.description: The linked account's user id, or `null` for an unlinked participant slot.

deactivatedAt.description: Non-null = deactivated; keeps their history, cannot join new transactions.

isLinked.description: Whether this slot maps to a real account.

balance.description: Σ paid − Σ owed, in the group's settlement currency. Positive = creditor.

SplitMode.description:
How the total is divided among the beneficiaries. `equal` = evenly; `amount` = each
beneficiary carries an explicit `rawAmount`; `share` = weights; `itemized` = the
beneficiaries live on the `items` (spending only).

TransactionListItem.description: One row of the transactions list.

amount.description: The original total, in the **entry** currency.

settlementAmount.description: The same total converted to the group's **settlement** currency.

isForeign.description: Whether the entry currency differs from settlement.

createdAt.description: The editable, backdatable **real-world date** of the transaction.

nextCursor.description: Pass back as `cursor` to fetch the next page. `null` = last page.

amountPaid.description: What this member paid, in the **entry** currency.

amountOwed.description: The resolved amount this member owes, in the **settlement** currency.

amount.description: The item's amount, in the **entry** currency.

DetailCharge.description: A charge / discount line applied on top of the itemized subtotal.

value.description:
A non-negative magnitude — the sign comes from `kind`. In `percent` mode it is
**basis points** (`1250` = 12.50%, max `10000`); in `absolute` mode it is
entry-currency minor units. This is deliberately not a money object: a percentage
is not an amount.

sortOrder.description: The order charges are applied in.

TransactionDetail.description: The full transaction, as returned by every read and every write.

amount.description: The original total, in the **entry** currency.

settlementAmount.description: The same total in the group's **settlement** currency.

deletedAt.description: Non-null = soft-deleted (excluded from balances and from the list).

shares.description:
The resolved per-member owed amounts — the source of truth for balances. Always
populated, including for an `itemized` transaction (where it is the aggregate of
the per-item shares plus the allocated charges).

amountPaid.description: Entry-currency minor units. The payers' amounts must sum to `amountTotal`.

ShareInput.description:
A beneficiary. Which optional field you send depends on the split mode: `rawAmount`
for `amount`, `shareWeight` for `share`, neither for `equal`.

rawAmount.description: `amount` split only: this member's share in entry-currency minor units. The raw amounts must sum to the total.

shareWeight.description: `share` split only: this member's weight. The weights must sum to more than zero.

ItemInput.description: One itemized line (spending only). Each item carries its own split.

amount.description: Entry-currency minor units. Must be greater than zero.

ChargeInput.description: A charge / discount applied on top of the items subtotal (itemized only).

value.description: Basis points in `percent` mode (max `10000`), entry-currency minor units in `absolute` mode.

date.description:
The **real-world date** (`YYYY-MM-DD`) the money moved — editable and backdatable,
but never in the future. Defaults to today when omitted.

amountTotal.description: The total in `currency`'s minor units. Must be greater than zero unless `itemized`.

currency.description: The **entry** currency — what was actually spent. May differ from the group's settlement currency.

exchangeRate.description:
Settlement-currency units per 1 unit of `currency`, as a **string** (up to 6 decimal
places, greater than zero) so no float ever touches the money math. Exactly `"1"`
when `currency` is the settlement currency.

amountTotalSettlement.description:
`amountTotal` converted to the group's settlement currency, in ITS minor units.
Caller-supplied and re-checked server-side (see the rule above) — a mismatch is a `422`.

beneficiaries.description: Who the spending is for. Empty for `itemized` (the beneficiaries live on the items).

items.description: Itemized lines. Required (non-empty) for `splitMode: itemized`, `[]` otherwise.

SettleUpInput.description: A settle-up payment from the debtor `from` to the creditor `to`.

to.description: The receiving member's id (the creditor). Must differ from `from`.

amount.description: The payment, in the group's **settlement-currency** minor units.

Error.description:
The one error envelope. `code` is the stable, machine-readable contract — branch on
it, not on `message`.

message.description: A human-readable explanation. Not a stable contract.

details.description:
Structured context, present on some codes. `validation_error` carries
`{ formErrors, fieldErrors }`; `rate_limited` carries
`{ scope, limit, windowSeconds, retryAfterSeconds }`; `conflict` carries
`{ reason: "key_reused" | "in_progress" }`.

amountTotalSettlement: The settlement total does not match the converted amount

BadRequest.description: `bad_request` — the request body or an opaque cursor could not be parsed.

error.message: The request body is not valid JSON.

Unauthorized.description: `unauthorized` — missing, invalid, expired or revoked key (all collapse to this).

ForbiddenScope.description: `forbidden_scope` — a read-only key attempted a write.

error.message: This API key does not have write access.

NotFound.description:
`not_found` — the resource does not exist **or** the caller has no access to it. The
two are deliberately conflated so existence never leaks.

error.message: The requested resource was not found.

Conflict.description:
`conflict` — an `Idempotency-Key` was reused with a different body
(`details.reason: "key_reused"`), or a concurrent retry with the same key is still
running (`details.reason: "in_progress"`).

error.message: This Idempotency-Key was already used with a different request body.

ValidationError.description: `validation_error` — a rule failed. `details.fieldErrors` names the offending fields.

amountTotalSettlement: The settlement total does not match the converted amount

RateLimited.description:
`rate_limited` — too many requests for this key. Read and write have independent
per-key counters (100/60s and 20/60s), behind a combined 150/60s backstop. Back off
for `Retry-After` seconds.

InternalError.description: `internal_error` — something broke on our side. Safe to retry (with an `Idempotency-Key` on creates).
